commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ad5225951176e93e46b631e5e2fa5a1cbcbbd80e)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds shared structured tool-result extraction/redaction and applies it across core provider replay, agent transport streams, bundled Google/xAI transport paths, and focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +341, Tests +849, Config +5, Other +2. Total +1197 across 30 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current-main source shows text-only extraction before placeholder fallback, and #96857 includes live Discord and Telegram evidence for ordinary tool outputs degrading to `(see attached image)`.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow mechanical repair can add the missing private SDK owner mapping and loader test without changing the product direction of the fix.

target.title: fix(llm): preserve structured tool result text across providers
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Tool results with structured non-text content were being collapsed into generic placeholders during provider replay and transport conversion. In practice, structured non-media blocks could become `(see attached image)`, `(see attached media)`, or empty/no-output text, which loses useful tool output and can mislead the model.

This PR preserves explicit text first, serializes structured non-media blocks as redacted JSON text, and keeps media-only fallbacks type-aware.


## Current Head

`f471e8e801c4e5bfaaf7e766e9d9146f246e46d5`

## Why This Change Was Made

The canonical user-facing problem is tracked in #96857. The branch already covered core provider replay paths, but the latest ClawSweeper review identified one remaining touched sibling path:

- `extensions/google/transport-stream.ts`: the bundled Google transport still built `functionResponse.response.output` from explicit `text` blocks or a media placeholder only, so structured-only non-media tool results could still become empty output.

This head closes that Google transport gap without importing core `src/**` helpers into plugin production code. The extension now has boundary-safe structured extraction for Google tool results before media/no-output fallback.

## User Impact

- Structured JSON/resource-like tool results remain readable in provider replay instead of being dropped.
- Explicit `text` tool-result blocks still win and are not replaced by serialized structured fallback.
- Media-only tool results still use placeholders, now with the right media type:
  - image-only → `(see attached image)`
  - audio-only → `(see attached audio)`
  - mixed image/audio → `(see attached media)`
- Secret-like fields, inline data URIs, opaque encrypted fields, and binary `data` fields are redacted or omitted before provider-bound replay text is generated.
- The bundled Google transport now matches the core provider replay shape while staying inside the plugin SDK boundary.

## Implementation

- Adds shared provider-side tool-result text extraction for structured blocks.
- Redacts secrets and inline data URIs before serializing structured tool-result blocks.
- Omits opaque/binary fields instead of replaying raw payloads.
- Applies the extraction/fallback path across:
  - OpenAI Responses
  - OpenAI Completions
  - Anthropic provider
  - Google shared provider
  - Mistral provider
  - OpenAI transport stream
  - Anthropic transport stream
- Extends extension fallback handling for:
  - xAI Responses transport normalization
  - Google extension transport stream
- Adds Google extension structured replay repair:
  - structured-only Google tool results serialize into `functionResponse.response.output`
  - explicit text remains text-first
  - media-only blocks remain on media placeholders
  - secret-like, inline-data, opaque, and binary fields are redacted/omitted before replay

## Regression Coverage

Added or updated focused coverage for:

- `src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts`
  - explicit text wins
  - structured non-media blocks serialize as JSON text
  - media-only blocks are omitted from text extraction
  - image/audio/mixed media placeholder selection
  - inline data URI and binary-field redaction
- `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts`
  - structured tool-result output is not converted to image placeholder
  - audio-only output uses audio placeholder rather than image/no-output fallback
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
  - structured output is preserved rather than collapsed to image placeholder
- `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts`
  - structured blocks are serialized
  - structured blocks are preserved alongside images
- `extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts`
  - structured-only Google tool-result output becomes `functionResponse.response.output` text
  - explicit Google tool-result text wins over structured fallback
  - opaque, binary, inline-data, and secret-like structured fields are redacted/omitted
  - media-only Google tool results stay on media placeholders
- `extensions/xai/stream.test.ts`
  - audio-only function output uses audio placeholder

## Evidence

### Review result addressed

Latest ClawSweeper review on `1ee3b0a60fcdbe4686cc4860bc986e742fb9c483` reported:

- Finding: `[P2] Serialize structured Google tool results before fallback`
- Location: `extensions/google/transport-stream.ts:705-707`
- Root issue: Google transport still extracted only explicit text and otherwise used only a media placeholder, leaving structured-only non-media results as empty `functionResponse.response.output`.

This head addresses that finding with extension-boundary-safe structured extraction and focused Google transport regression coverage.

### Current branch author-side validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot`
  - Result: passed, 1 file / 72 tests.
  - Covers the new Google structured-only replay path, explicit-text-first behavior, media-only placeholders, and redaction/omission behavior.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt extensions/google/transport-stream.ts extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts`
  - Result: completed for both changed files.
- `node --check extensions/google/transport-stream.ts`
  - Result: passed before the focused test run.
- `node --check extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts`
  - Result: passed before the focused test run.
- `git diff --check`
  - Result: passed before commit.

### Latest patch-quality update

Current head `f471e8e801c4e5bfaaf7e766e9d9146f246e46d5` keeps the existing provider replay evidence intact and addresses the latest ClawSweeper patch-quality/security finding by removing the Google extension-local structured serializer copy:

- `src/plugin-sdk/provider-transport-runtime.ts` exposes the shared structured tool-result text extraction and media-placeholder helpers as a plugin-safe seam.
- `extensions/google/transport-stream.ts` reuses those shared SDK helpers for Google `functionResponse.response.output`, so Google now follows the same redaction/truncation/media fallback contract as the core provider replay paths.
- `src/logging/redact.ts` keeps the shared structured redaction contract broad enough for the reviewed sensitive key families before provider replay text is serialized, while preserving uppercase diagnostic `code` values such as `SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED`.
- `extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts`, `src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts`, and `src/logging/redact.test.ts` cover structured-only Google output, explicit-text-first behavior, media-only placeholders, opaque/binary/inline-data omission, the reviewed sensitive field names (`privateKey`, `private_key`, `cookie`, `set-cookie`, `jwt`, `session`, `code`, `signature`, generic `key`, and payment fields), and the diagnostic-code non-regression case.
- `docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md` and `docs/plugins/sdk-migration.md` document that `plugin-sdk/provider-transport-runtime` now includes tool-result replay helpers.

Author-side validation for this head:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/redact.test.ts src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot` passed, covering 3 focused files / 192 tests.
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/google/transport-stream.ts extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-transport-runtime.ts` passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors.
- `node --check extensions/google/transport-stream.ts` passed.
- `node --check extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts` passed.
- `node --check src/plugin-sdk/provider-transport-runtime.ts` passed.
- `node --check src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts` passed.
- `node --check src/logging/redact.ts` passed.
- `node --check src/logging/redact.test.ts` passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt extensions/google/transport-stream.ts extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-transport-runtime.ts src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts src/logging/redact.ts` completed.
- `pnpm docs:list` completed and the affected SDK docs were checked.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Current-head GitHub Actions evidence:

- `Real behavior proof` passed for `f471e8e801c4e5bfaaf7e766e9d9146f246e46d5`: <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28456351010/job/84332098351>

### Existing provider replay proof retained from the previous detailed PR body

The previous current-head evidence remains relevant to the core provider replay slice and is repeated here so the PR body stays globally consistent instead of losing proof while the branch advances:

- Current-head Real behavior proof checks previously passed for provider replay/redaction coverage:
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28379436171/job/84077730201>
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28379481457/job/84077892770>
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28379522284/job/84078045029>
- Earlier current-head proof after body refresh also passed:
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28378605470/job/84074771813>
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28378717544/job/84075168883>
- The redacted provider replay artifact demonstrated the current-head replay wire shapes for:
  - OpenAI Responses: structured JSON reached `function_call_output.output` as text instead of `(see attached image)`.
  - Anthropic provider replay: structured tool-result text was serialized into the provider-bound replay content instead of becoming an image placeholder.
  - Mistral provider replay: structured tool-result text was serialized into the provider-bound replay content instead of becoming an image placeholder.
  - Redaction behavior: secret-like input was redacted; inline data URIs, binary data, and opaque encrypted fields were omitted or replaced with redacted placeholders before provider replay.
- A later PR-head Real behavior proof check on `1ee3b0a60fcdbe4686cc4860bc986e742fb9c483` passed before the body-only Evidence-section gate failed:
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28440633789/job/84277435867>

### CI failures observed before this update

- `Real behavior proof` failed because the PR body no longer had an authored `Evidence` section:
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28440711254/job/84277710263>
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28440727959/job/84277767312>
- `QA Smoke CI` failed in `Runtime inventory drift check` with a gateway timeout after `config.patch` and hot reload, not in the provider/media placeholder tests:
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28440636120/job/84277548816>
  - Artifact summary: 80 scenarios passed, 1 failed; failing scenario was `runtime-inventory-drift-check`.

This body update restores authored Evidence, and the code commit retriggers CI for the Google transport repair.


### CI follow-up repair

Current head `4734b5c558fe1f976ef35b6df9d3243a92a751f5` addresses the latest CI failures observed after the Google redaction repair:

- Updated the intentional Plugin SDK public surface budget for the two new provider transport replay helpers (`extractToolResultText` and `describeToolResultMediaPlaceholder`).
- Preserved internal trajectory manifest warning codes such as `invalid-runtime-event` / `cyclic-session-branch` and `$WORKSPACE_DIR/*.jsonl` source-file placeholders while continuing to redact opaque provider/OAuth `code` values.

Author-side validation for this follow-up head:

- `node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check` passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/redact.test.ts src/trajectory/export.test.ts test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot` passed.
- `node --check src/logging/redact.ts` and `node --check src/logging/redact.test.ts` passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt src/logging/redact.ts src/logging/redact.test.ts scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs` completed.
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/logging/redact.ts src/logging/redact.test.ts extensions/google/transport-stream.ts extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-transport-runtime.ts` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.


### Latest CI follow-up repair

Current head `fd87d3e8d79598dbf15297765f3de575008ff1b9` addresses the latest CI failures after the previous follow-up:

- Broadened the internal diagnostic warning-code preservation to cover additional trajectory warning slugs while still excluding OAuth/provider-style opaque code values from the allowlist.
- Kept the Plugin SDK surface budget aligned with the two intentional provider transport replay helper exports.

Author-side validation for this head:

- `node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check` passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/redact.test.ts src/trajectory/export.test.ts test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot` passed.
- `node --check src/logging/redact.ts` and `node --check src/logging/redact.test.ts` passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt src/logging/redact.ts src/logging/redact.test.ts scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs` completed.
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/logging/redact.ts src/logging/redact.test.ts extensions/google/transport-stream.ts extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-transport-runtime.ts` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.

## Risk

Low to moderate:

- Text tool results keep existing explicit text behavior.
- Structured non-media data is serialized with redaction/truncation before provider conversion.
- Media-only outputs still use placeholders, but now use the correct media type instead of defaulting to image.
- Binary payloads and opaque encrypted fields are not replayed as raw text.
- The Google extension implementation stays within plugin SDK boundaries and does not deep-import core provider helpers.

## Rollback

Revert this PR to restore the previous provider/transport placeholder behavior.
target.author: snowzlmbot

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

Low to moderate:

- Text tool results keep existing explicit text behavior.
- Structured non-media data is serialized with redaction/truncation before provider conversion.
- Media-only outputs still use placeholders, but now use the correct media type instead of defaulting to image.
- Binary payloads and opaque encrypted fields are not replayed as raw text.
- The Google extension implementation stays within plugin SDK boundaries and does not deep-import core provider helpers.

## Rollback

Revert this PR to restore the previous provider/transport placeholder behavior.
target.author: snowzlmbot

commands.reason: matching ClawSweeper response comment already exists

commands.author: snowzlmbot

target.title: feat(mxc): add Windows MXC sandbox backend
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

OpenClaw does not currently have an official installable MXC sandbox backend for Windows hosts that can run sandboxed commands through Microsoft eXecution Containers. This leaves MXC-capable Windows environments without a first-party plugin path for ProcessContainer-backed execution and filesystem bridge operations.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds an official OpenShell-style external `mxc` sandbox plugin package (`@openclaw/mxc-sandbox`) with Windows-only readiness checks, plugin-owned MXC SDK dependency ownership, ProcessContainer payload construction, a plugin-side launcher, host-backed filesystem bridge support, sandbox policy loading, and targeted tests.

The plugin source lives under `extensions/mxc`, but it is packaged as an installable plugin (`bundledDist: false`) so the MXC SDK/native dependency is owned by the plugin package instead of an unshipped bundled `node_modules` tree. The backend uses the stable current MXC `0.7.0-alpha` schema and keeps sandbox defaults conservative: curated environments, headless UI, per-exec temp directories, absolute binary overrides, host-derived Windows roots, read-only default workspace access for `none`/`ro`, and fail-closed unsupported policy handling.

## User Impact

Users on MXC-capable Windows hosts can install the official MXC sandbox plugin, restart the Gateway, configure `sandbox.backend: "mxc"`, and run OpenClaw sandboxed commands through MXC. Non-Windows or unsupported Windows hosts remain unaffected because the plugin stays dormant when readiness probes fail.

Install command:

```bash
openclaw plugins install @openclaw/mxc-sandbox
```

## Evidence

- `pnpm test:extension mxc -- --reporter=verbose` — passed.
- `pnpm test extensions\mxc test\scripts\generate-npm-shrinkwrap.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` — passed.
- `pnpm test:changed` — passed.
- `pnpm release:plugins:npm:check -- --plugins @openclaw/mxc-sandbox` — passed; publishable plugin metadata looks OK.
- `pnpm release:plugins:clawhub:check -- --plugins @openclaw/mxc-sandbox` — passed; publishable plugin metadata looks OK.
- `pnpm check` — passed, including import cycle check with 0 runtime value cycles.
- `pnpm build` — passed.
- `node scripts\lib\plugin-npm-runtime-build.mjs extensions\mxc` plus package-manifest `npm pack --dry-run` — passed; package includes `dist/index.js`, `dist/mxc-spawn-launcher.mjs`, and MXC SDK binaries including `node_modules/@microsoft/mxc-sdk/bin/x64/wxc-exec.exe`.
- `pnpm deps:changes:report -- --base-ref origin/main --markdown <session>/dependency-changes.md --json <session>/dependency-changes.json` — generated dependency report.

Real Windows MXC package proof:

- Crabbox provider: `ssh`
- Lease/id: `static_10-127-3-94`
- Target: `windows-normal`
- Remote Node/npm: `node v26.4.0`, `npm 11.17.0`
- Built and packed `@openclaw/mxc-sandbox@2026.6.10` on the Windows host.
- Installed published `openclaw@2026.6.10` plus the local packed plugin in a fresh temp project.
- Verified package-owned runtime dependency path:
  - `PROOF:installedOpenClaw=2026.6.10`
  - `PROOF:installedPlugin=2026.6.10`
  - `PROOF:mxcSdk=0.7.0`
  - `PROOF:wxcExists=True ...\node_modules\@openclaw\mxc-sandbox\node_modules\@microsoft\mxc-sdk\bin\x64\wxc-exec.exe`
- Registered and ran backend `mxc`:
  - `PROOF:registeredServices=mxc-sandbox-cleanup`
  - `PROOF:backendId=mxc`
  - sandbox command: `echo MXC_PROOF_OK && cd`
  - `PROOF:exit=0`
  - `PROOF:stdout=MXC_PROOF_OK |...\workspace|`
  - `PROOF:stderr=`

User-facing plugin install proof on the same Windows host:

- Installed published `openclaw@2026.6.10` in an isolated temp project.
- Ran `node <published-openclaw>/dist/entry.js plugins install <local @openclaw/mxc-sandbox tgz> --force --pin`.
- Install succeeded:
  - `PROOF3:installExit=0`
  - install output included `Installing plugin dependencies…`, `Installed plugin: mxc`, and `Restart the gateway to load plugins.`
  - `PROOF3:configExists=True`
  - `PROOF3:configHasMxc=True`
  - `PROOF3:pluginRoot=...\state\extensions\mxc`
  - `PROOF3:installedPlugin=2026.6.10`

Workspace access proof after review 4588711229:

- Static SSH Windows host: `provider=ssh`, lease `static_10-127-3-94`, target `windows-normal`.
- `workspaceAccess: "none"` now keeps the MXC workdir read-only rather than read-write by default:
  - `PROOF4:backendId=mxc`
  - `PROOF4:workspaceAccess=none`
  - `PROOF4:workdirReadonly=true`
  - `PROOF4:workdirReadwrite=false`
- Command execution still succeeds with the read-only default workspace policy:
  - sandbox command: `echo MXC_NONE_PROOF_OK && cd`
  - `PROOF4:exit=0`
  - `PROOF4:stdout=MXC_NONE_PROOF_OK |...\workspace|`
  - `PROOF4:stderr=`

Full `pnpm test` was attempted earlier per CONTRIBUTING, but the broad local suite hit unrelated existing non-MXC failures in UI/TUI/agent runtime tests before completion. The changed/MXC-specific lanes above passed cleanly.

Copilot autoreview was attempted on the latest review-fix head. It produced a finding about `@microsoft/mxc-sdk@0.7.0` re-serializing config into `--config-base64` for `wxc-exec`; local source inspection confirmed that this is the current upstream SDK transport behavior and that the SDK exposes no non-argv transport for `spawnSandboxFromConfig`. No local OpenClaw-only patch is available without an upstream SDK/executor transport change.

## Notes

- Contributor changelog edit was removed per CONTRIBUTING.
- `.github/labeler.yml` maps `extensions/mxc/**` to `plugin: mxc`.
- Upstream label verification found no existing `plugin: mxc` label; label creation requires maintainer permissions.
- Dependency Graph Guard still requires maintainer/security `/allow-dependencies-change` approval for the current head SHA 7dc546ccb7ffc27830bbe1c932f58640662c52c7.
